original_abstract: In this research, we introduce BEVDepth, a high-performance 3D object detector designed for camera-based Bird’s-Eye-View (BEV) perception. Our approach is motivated by the observation that while depth is a fundamental component of camera-based 3D detection, depth estimation in existing methods remains surprisingly inadequate. BEVDepth addresses this deficiency by incorporating explicit depth supervision during training and introducing a camera-aware depth estimation module to enhance predictive accuracy. Additionally, we design a novel Depth Refinement Module to mitigate the spatial errors introduced by imprecise feature unprojection. When integrated with a customized Efficient Voxel Pooling strategy and a multi-frame fusion mechanism, BEVDepth sets a new benchmark on the challenging nuScenes test set with 60.9% NDS. Notably, this represents the first time a camera-only model has surpassed the 60% NDS threshold while maintaining high computational efficiency. Code and models have been made publicly available.
humanized_abstract: Currently 3D object detection for camera-based Bird’s-Eye-View (BEV) perception is based on projecting 2D camera features into the BEV space. While depth is an essential component in camera-based 3D detection, the depth estimates from previous works are surprisingly poor. In this paper, we propose a high-performance 3D object detector named BEVDepth, where we enforce explicit depth supervision and design a camera-aware depth estimation head to significantly improve the depth accuracy. Meanwhile, a Depth Refinement Module is introduced to eliminate the drawbacks caused by inaccurate feature unprojection. By combining it with a customized Efficient Voxel Pooling (EVP) and multi-frame fusion, BEVDepth achieves 60.9% NDS on the nuScenes test set, which is the first camera-only model to achieve over 60% NDS and is still very efficient. Our code and models will be open-sourced.

By combining it with a customized Efficient Voxel Pooling (EVP) and multi-frame fusion, BEVDepth achieves 60.9% NDS on the nuScenes test set, which is the first camera-only model to achieve over 60% NDS and is still very efficient. Our code and models will be open-sourced.
